details: scripts/postinstall.js is heavily obfuscated, using mangled hex-style identifiers (e.g. _0x2d7b51, _0x5cc22b) that are characteristic of automated JavaScript obfuscators. Obfuscation in a lifecycle script that runs automatically on `npm install` is a strong red flag — there is no legitimate reason for a postinstall hook to hide its behavior — but the specific runtime behavior (network destinations, file reads, exec targets) was not traced in this run, so the concrete installer-harm mechanism is not yet established. Routing for human review to deobfuscate the postinstall and confirm whether it performs exfiltration, dropper, or credential-harvest actions before publishing a block verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
